description: Routing fixture for verify-before-complete after the precision retune. Positive cases assert the circuit-breaker situations still route here: delegated/subagent work, high-risk changes (auth/migrations/infra/security/broad refactor), missing or stale evidence, and explicit completion audits. Negative controls assert that routine, low-risk completion phrasing ('renamed it, tests pass, done') no longer routes here — that phrasing is exactly what the old broad description over-caught, firing the skill after nearly every chunk of work.
notes: Scored by run_trigger_evals under the ranking model (default). Positive cases: verify-before-complete must be the top-scoring skill. Negative controls use the matches-nothing model (empty trigger): verify-before-complete must stay under the 0.30 floor, i.e. routine completion no longer pulls the skill. These negatives are the red step — they fail against the pre-retune description whose trigger words were the universal 'fixed / passing / done / complete'.

cases.prompt: Before you claim this is done, show me the evidence that it actually passes.
The subagent reports it finished the migration. Verify that delegated work locally before we call it complete.
I reworked the auth and session migration across several modules. Confirm it is actually verified with fresh evidence before I merge.
You are about to claim this is complete, but no verification has run since the last edit — the passing evidence is stale. Confirm it with fresh proof before we call it done.
Use $verify-before-complete to gate this completion claim on real evidence.
Renamed the helper and the unit tests pass. Done.
Fixed the typo in the README. Ready to commit.
Updated the config comment. That change is complete.
Bumped the lint dependency and the build is green. All set.
